global.legalDisclaimer: The admissibility of evidence remains subject to evaluation by the competent authority under applicable law.

home.pageTitle: Legal Infrastructure for Digital Evidence Preservation
home.heroTitle: Digital Evidentiary Preservation with Verifiable Chain of Custody
home.heroSubtitle: Technological infrastructure dedicated to the structured organization of digital assets with verifiable chain of custody, documented technical integrity and support for potential notarial formalization upon request by the holder.
home.introTitle: How to Preserve Digital Evidence with Legal Certainty
home.introP1: Digital evidentiary preservation consists of the structured and chronological organization of digital assets with potential legal relevance.
home.introP2: It operates preferably in the pre-litigation phase, structuring records before judicial, administrative or arbitral proceedings.
home.introP3: Admissibility of evidence remains subject to evaluation by the competent authority.
home.pillar1Desc: Structured chronological recording of all events related to the digital asset.
home.pillar2Desc: Technical mechanisms allowing future verification of integrity without exposing the content.
home.pillar3Desc: Possibility of notarial deed issuance based on previously preserved assets upon request.
home.h2Main: Technical Organization of Evidence with Probative Capacity
home.h2Secondary: Infrastructure Based on the Brazilian Code of Civil Procedure
home.home_applicability_desc: Digital evidentiary preservation may be used for judicial proceedings, administrative defenses, arbitration procedures, internal investigations and notarial formalizations.

preservation.title: How to Preserve Digital Evidence with Integrity and Documented Chain of Custody
preservation.p1: Preserving digital evidence requires technical mechanisms capable of chronologically and structurally recording events related to the asset.
preservation.p2: The digital chain of custody documents deposit, identifier generation and timestamp registration.
preservation.p3: Technical organization contributes to greater documentary clarity in proceedings.

legalBasis.title: Legal Basis of Digital Evidence Preservation under Brazilian Law
legalBasis.p1: Brazilian law admits documentary evidence produced electronically provided its integrity and authenticity are demonstrated.
legalBasis.p2: Structured digital records may support the formation of judicial conviction.
legalBasis.p3: Formalization through notarial deed may reinforce chronological documentation.
legalBasis.h2Main: Legal Basis for Electronic Evidence Admissibility
legalBasis.h3CivilProcedure: Code of Civil Procedure — Arts. 369, 422 and 439

security.title: Integrity Architecture Applied to Digital Evidentiary Preservation
security.p1: Digital assets are protected by encryption and authentication mechanisms linked to the holder’s digital certificate.
security.p2: Preserved content is not accessible to third parties.
security.p3: Complementary integrity verification records may be generated without exposing content.
security.eNotarialDesc: Identity validation through the official Brazilian notary platform ensuring public faith.
security.nonRepudiationDesc: Mechanisms preventing denial of authorship or alteration.
security.encryptionDesc: High-standard encryption algorithms protecting assets during transmission and storage.
security.blockchainDesc: Distributed and immutable ledger ensuring traceability.
security.chainOfCustodyDesc: Complete and unalterable history of actions performed on each asset.
security.evidentialValidityDesc: Support for admissibility in administrative and judicial proceedings.
security.reliabilityDesc: Legal reliability depends on integrity, traceability and authorship control.
security.ctaDesc: Access the platform and explore our evidentiary preservation infrastructure.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.step1Desc: The process begins with the identification of the rights holder, which may involve notarial validation to reinforce legal security.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Following authentication, the digital asset is deposited and assigned a verifiable technical identifier.
howItWorks.step3Desc: All events become part of a structured chronological record documenting preservation.
howItWorks.step4Desc: Access remains restricted to the rights holder through authentication via digital certificate.
howItWorks.subtitle: A structured process for evidentiary preservation of digital assets with verifiable records.
howItWorks.processDescription: Tutela Digital® has developed a streamlined process that combines the usability of a modern digital platform with the legal rigor required to ensure the legal validity of custody. In four stages, you initiate the protection of your digital assets with public faith and an auditable chain of custody.
howItWorks.stepPlatformRegistrationDesc: Access the platform and complete your initial registration with basic identification information.
howItWorks.stepPlanSelectionDesc: Select the plan appropriate to your custody requirements and storage capacity needs.
howItWorks.stepNotaryInterviewDesc: Schedule and complete the interview with a notary public via the e-Notariado system for identity validation and public faith.
howItWorks.stepDepositAndRecordDesc: The digital asset undergoes technical integrity preservation mechanisms and timestamp registration, formally initiating its digital chain of custody.
howItWorks.legalValidityDesc: The admissibility of evidence is subject to review by the competent authority. The infrastructure ensures structured preservation and technical documentation of the digital asset.
howItWorks.ctaDesc: Access the platform and begin the protection process for your digital assets.

government.heroTitle: Digital assets in the public sector: existence is not enough — control, traceability and evidentiary integrity are essential
government.heroSubtitle: Documents, evidence, contracts and digital records are public assets. Without governance structure, they lose traceability, integrity and evidentiary value. Tutela Digital® delivers the technical and legal layer required by public administration.
government.introTitle: The State's digital assets require structure
government.introP1: Digital assets in the public sector range from electronic administrative contracts and disciplinary evidence to confidential databases and public policy records.
government.introP2: Without governance, these assets may legally exist but become unusable — undermining transparency, accountability and legal certainty.
government.asideContent: Article 37 of the Brazilian Federal Constitution establishes the principles of legality, impersonality, morality, publicity and efficiency.
government.desafioTitle: Where the risk lies for public administration
government.desafioLead: Lack of governance transforms public records into risk elements: loss of control, evidentiary fragility and regulatory exposure.
government.sem1: Access lost or contested in proceedings
government.sem2: Violation of the Access to Information Law
government.sem3: Administrative irregularity due to lack of traceability
government.com2: Compliance with LGPD and data processing records
government.com3: Chain of custody aligned with evidentiary standards
government.normativeTitle: Regulatory framework: legal requirements for digital asset governance
government.whatChangesLead: Tutela Digital® transforms digital records into controllable assets with verifiable integrity.
government.useCasesLead: Tutela Digital® ensures integrity and legal certainty.
government.ctaSubtitle: Contact us to learn more about public sector solutions.
government.content: Governance, traceability and evidentiary integrity for public digital assets.

companies.heroTitle: Corporate digital assets: existing on a server is not the same as existing in law
companies.heroSubtitle: Electronic contracts, emails, decision records, intellectual property and corporate evidence only have legal value when preserved with integrity, traceability and a verifiable chain of custody. Tutela Digital® provides this structure.
companies.badge3: CPC · Consumer Law · Civil Internet Framework
companies.introTitle: Corporate digital assets require legal structure
companies.introP1: In the corporate environment, digital assets include electronic contracts, internal and external communications, corporate decision records, compliance documents, digital intellectual property and evidentiary records in labor and civil disputes.
companies.introP2: The most common mistake is confusing storage with custody. A file stored on a server may not legally exist without verifiable integrity, timestamping and a documented chain of custody.
companies.asideContent: Article 422 of the Brazilian Code of Civil Procedure requires verifiable authenticity. Article 439 establishes that evidentiary weight depends on accompanying elements.
companies.normativeTitle: What the law requires for corporate digital asset management
companies.riskTitle: Where the risk lies for the company
companies.riskLead: Lack of governance transforms evidence into legal liabilities.
companies.risk1: Electronic contracts challenged due to lack of integrity
companies.risk2: LGPD fines due to inability to prove compliance
companies.benefit1: Verifiable integrity with hash and timestamp

individuals.heroSubtitle: Legal custody of digital evidence for protection of rights.
individuals.section1Content: Professional evidentiary preservation technology accessible to individuals.
individuals.benefit1Content: Preserve evidence of harassment or violations.
individuals.useCasesContent: Applicable in personal disputes and rights protection.

hero.title: Digital Asset Management in Contemporary Law
hero.subtitle: A legal approach to custody, governance, and succession of digital assets with verifiable integrity
hero.cta: Understand how to structure the protection of your digital assets

problem.title: The Legal Problem
problem.text: The increasing digitization of assets has created a new class of property — digital assets — whose existence is often not reflected in traditional legal structures. The absence of inventory, custody, and proper documentation may compromise access, transfer, and evidentiary use.

scale.title: The Scale of the Problem
items: 12 million individuals in Brazil holding crypto assets
Only 12% of companies have formal custody policies
60 million individuals exposed to digital assets

taxonomy.title: What Are Digital Assets (Legal Perspective)
items: Non-fungible tokens (NFTs) as intangible movable assets
taxonomy.note: Loss of credentials may compromise access to the asset itself.

regulatory.title: Regulatory Gaps
items: No consolidated legal framework for digital inventory
Limited institutional standards for digital custody

succession.title: Digital Succession
succession.text: Lack of structured guidance may lead to loss of access, difficulties in estate procedures, and potential professional liability.

infrastructure.title: Legal-Digital Infrastructure
infrastructure.text: Proper digital asset management requires identification of the holder, formal expression of intent, cryptographic evidence, verifiable integrity, and an auditable trail.

implementation.title: How This Structure Is Implemented
items: Formal consent recorded via digital notarization

context.title: Regulatory Context
context.text: Global regulatory convergence (e.g., MiCA in the EU) indicates increasing requirements for governance of digital assets.

ctaFinal.text: Structuring digital asset governance is no longer optional.
ctaFinal.cta: Learn how to implement this approach in practice
